name: Standing Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Hardest in the stretched bottom position; pause there.
rom_notes: Drop into a full stretch at the bottom, rise to full plantarflexion.
cues: Straight (or nearly straight) knee to bias the gastrocnemius
Full stretch at the bottom, full contraction at the top
execution_steps: Step under the shoulder pads with the balls of your feet on the platform, heels off the edge
Stand tall with knees straight or nearly straight and lift the weight off the stops
Lower your heels for a deep stretch at the bottom
Rise onto your toes to full plantarflexion and squeeze
Pause in the stretch each rep; do not bounce
good_when: You want to load the gastrocnemius with the knee straight
You want heavy, progressable calf work in a dedicated machine
Training the calf through a full stretch-to-contraction range
bad_when: You want to bias the soleus (bend the knee with a seated raise instead)
Standing balance or lower-back tolerance is a limitation (use the leg press)
